# Project Guidelines

## Architecture

- Treat root frontend files as the source of truth: `index.html`, `pages/`, `assets/`, `src/`, and `prisma/`.
- Do not hand-edit `public/`; it is regenerated by `npm run build` through `scripts/build-public.js`.
- The Express app is duplicated in both `src/` and `backend/src/` for different deploy paths. When changing routes, auth, middleware, config, or shared services, mirror the change in both trees unless you are intentionally consolidating them.
- Prisma schema and seed files are also duplicated in `prisma/` and `backend/prisma/`; keep them aligned.

## Build And Test

- Frontend/public sync: `npm run build`
- Vercel build/deploy: `npm run vercel:build`, `npm run vercel:build:prod`, `npm run vercel:deploy`, `npm run vercel:deploy:prod`
- Backend local dev: `cd backend && npm run dev`
- Backend tests: `cd backend && npm test`
- Backend schema and data tools: `cd backend && npm run db:push`, `npm run db:seed`, `npm run db:studio`

## Conventions

- On frontend pages, prefer `window.OSKSession` for API base resolution, session sync, and CSRF token handling.
- Shared page chrome lives in `assets/js/header.js`, `assets/js/footer.js`, `assets/js/session.js`, and `assets/js/cloudinary-assets.js`; update those helpers instead of re-copying header/footer logic into pages.
- Keep root/source files and generated or duplicated copies consistent: edit the source page or source backend first, then regenerate or mirror.
- Preserve existing static HTML style on the site pages; most pages keep markup, styles, and behavior inline instead of using a framework.

## References

- See `CLAUDE.md` for repo layout, local run commands, and deployment context.
- See `README.md` for product features, roles, API overview, and environment expectations.
- See `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-03-29-shared-header.md` when changing shared header behavior.
